items.title: RWS launches Language Weaver Pro
items.summary:
The AIM-listed language technology company says its new model, the largest dedicated translation model in production, ranked first in 31 of 32 languages in internal benchmarking against DeepL and Gemini. The model is natively integrated into the Trados portfolio. The problem RWS is describing is a real one in enterprise translation: the tools that move […]
This story continues at The Next Web

items.title: Introducing the OpenAI Safety Bug Bounty program
items.summary: OpenAI launches a Safety Bug Bounty program to identify AI abuse and safety risks, including agentic vulnerabilities, prompt injection, and data exfiltration.

items.title: Google launches Lyria 3 Pro music generation model
items.summary: Google is launching Lyria 3 Pro, an upgraded music model that generates longer, more customizable tracks, as it expands AI music tools across Gemini, enterprise products, and other services.

items.title: Apple’s New Siri With ChatGPT-Like Features Could Be Coming Soon
items.summary:
Apple is reportedly preparing a major Siri overhaul with chatbot-style features, deeper app awareness, and possible Google Gemini support later in 2026.
The post Apple’s New Siri With ChatGPT-Like Features Could Be Coming Soon appeared first on TechRepublic.

items.title: Meta launches new initiative to support entrepreneurship, drive AI adoption
items.summary: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg said in a memo to staff that small businesses have always been a big part of the company's business model, and that while tens of millions of entrepreneurs already use its platforms to grow and connect with customers, the company wants to do more in the space.

items.title: The AI Hype Index: AI goes to war
items.summary: AI is at war. Anthropic and the Pentagon feuded over how to weaponize Anthropic’s AI model Claude; then OpenAI swept the Pentagon off its feet with an “opportunistic and sloppy” deal. Users quit ChatGPT in droves. People marched through London in the biggest protest against AI to date. If you’re keeping score, Anthropic—the company founded…

items.title: Anthropic’s Claude Code gets ‘safer’ auto mode
items.summary: Anthropic has launched an "auto mode" for Claude Code, a new tool that lets AI make permissions-level decisions on users' behalf. The company says the feature offers vibe coders a safer alternative between constant handholding or giving the model dangerous levels of autonomy. Claude Code is capable of acting independently on users' behalf, a useful […]

items.title: OpenAI Enters Its Focus Era by Killing Sora
items.summary: As the ChatGPT-maker eyes an IPO, it's ditching Sora in favor of a unified AI assistant and enterprise coding tools.

items.title: OpenAI Sora is gone. The artists are still working.
items.summary:
Last September, when OpenAI quietly released the Sora 2 app to the public, the discourse around it was not quiet at all. Commentators who had spent months watching the model’s preview clips, the golden retriever bounding through autumn leaves, the Tokyo street scene that looked almost real, lined up to declare that something had shifted. […]
This story continues at The Next Web

items.title: Openreach expands collaboration with Google Cloud AI to plan its full-fibre rollout
items.summary:
The BT subsidiary, operator of the UK’s largest broadband network and the country’s second-largest commercial fleet, has built a digital twin of the UK’s transport and broadband infrastructure on Vertex AI, and is using Gemini Enterprise to cut engineering overhead by more than half. Openreach, the BT subsidiary that operates the UK’s largest broadband network, […]
This story continues at The Next Web

items.title: Senate Democrats are trying to ‘codify’ Anthropic’s red lines on autonomous weapons and mass surveillance
items.summary: Anthropic's fight with the Pentagon is expanding to Congress. Sen. Adam Schiff (D-CA) is working on a new bill to "codify" Anthropic's red lines and ensure humans make the ultimate decisions in questions of life and death, and Sen. Elissa Slotkin (D-MI) recently introduced a bill to limit the Defense Department's ability to use AI […]

items.title: Amazon acquires Fauna Robotics and its approachable humanoid Sprout
items.summary:
The deal, Amazon’s second robotics acquisition this month, brings a 50-pound, 3.5-foot bipedal robot called Sprout into the company’s portfolio, less than two months after Fauna launched it to research and development partners. Terms were not disclosed. The race to put a humanoid robot in every home has a new entrant. Amazon has acquired Fauna […]
This story continues at The Next Web
